data.title: Market volatility: Stocks, bonds and the dollar swing on news threatening Fed Chair Powell, prompting revival of the "sell America" trade.
data.description: A larger movement away from U.S. financial assets could reignite the "sell America" trend from last year, leading to significant changes in investment patterns.

data.title: Andreessen Horowitz secures $15B! Fed chair vs Trump drama! Crypto prices stable; btc at $90,600. Ripple gets FCA approval to expand in UK.
data.description: Major cryptocurrencies remain relatively stable as the market opens on Monday, with Bitcoin steady at $90,600, Ethereum up 1% at $3,110, Solana up 2% at $140, and XRP down 2% at $2.04. Notable movers include IP, up 20%, and XMR, up 15%, reaching an all-time high at $590. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell addressed criminal charges against him, attributing them to not aligning interest rate cuts with Trump's demands. Twitter's X announced plans for "smart cashtags" to display live crypto and stock prices. VanEck predicted Bitcoin could hit $53 million by 2050, driven by long-term adoption and growth assumptions. Andreessen Horowitz raised $15 billion for American Dynamism, focusing on AI and crypto. Ripple obtained FCA approval to expand crypto payments in the UK. BNY Mellon introduced tokenized deposits for institutional and digital-native clients. A new house bill aims to restrict prediction market use among lawmakers and officials. Tether froze $182 million in USDT associated with Venezuelan oil trades.

data.title: CHARBONE closes $3.1M private placement, specializes in UHP hydrogen and industrial gases.
data.description: In Brossard, Quebec, on January 12, 2026, Charbone Corporation (TSXV: CH; OTCQB: CHHYF; FSE: K47), a leading North American company in clean Ultra High Purity ("UHP") hydrogen and strategic industrial gases production and distribution, has announced...

data.title: Gold, Silver hit record highs amid Fed independence concerns; Gold at $4,620/oz, Silver at $86/oz representing 190% rally. Fed Chair Powell investigation causing market shift towards precious metals.
data.description: Gold and silver prices hit new highs on Monday, with gold reaching $4,620 an ounce and silver surging over 7% to $86 an ounce, marking a 190% rally in the past year. This surge was driven by reports of a criminal probe opened by the Department of Justice into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over renovation costs at the Fed's headquarters. Powell framed the investigation as a tactic to pressure the central bank into cutting interest rates, contrary to President Donald Trump's desires. Investors interpreted this as an attempt to challenge the Fed's independence, leading to a sell-off of Treasuries and a move towards precious metals.MACOM Technology's stock is also approaching key resistance levels, pointing towards a bullish trend.

data.title: US close to a trade deal with Taiwan to reduce tariffs. Taiwanese Semiconductor Manufacturing Corp. to build more plants in US.
data.description: As per the recent agreement, there will be a reduction in tariffs, with Taiwanese Semiconductor Manufacturing Corporation- Taiwan's leading chip manufacturer, committing to construct additional manufacturing facilities in the United States. This move will significantly contribute to boosting the manufacturing sector in the US and fostering better trade relations between the two nations.

data.title: Europe Fleet Management industry to reach 30.5 million units by 2029 – Commercial vehicle telematics trends and developments.
data.description: A recent report, "Fleet Management in Europe - 20th Edition," has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's portfolio. The report analyzes the future evolution of the European market for fleet management systems for commercial vehicles up to 2026 and beyond. As the twentieth edition of this report, it provides insights into the current trends and advancements in the commercial vehicle telematics sector. The report predicts that the number of active fleet management systems in use in Europe is anticipated to rise significantly.

data.title: Tennessee orders prediction platforms like Polymarket to close sports markets, refunding wagers.
data.description: Multiple major prediction market platforms, including Polymarket, have been directed to cease all sports-related markets in the state. The order mandates an immediate closure of such markets and necessitates the reimbursement of all pending wagers. This decision affects a significant number of users on these platforms who engaged in sports-related prediction activities. The directive underscores the regulatory oversight on prediction markets, particularly in the sports sector, impacting both the platforms and their participants.

China’s first domestically developed ton-class cargo drone, the Tianma-1000, has completed its maiden flight. Capable of carrying 1 tonne up to 1,800 km, it’s designed for logistics, disaster relief, and supply drops—even in hard-to-reach areas.

data.title: Technip Energies sets dates for reporting full-year 2025 and quarterly 2026 results.
data.description: In the latest report, it was revealed that the local government has allocated a total budget of $5 million to support small business recovery efforts in the area. The funding will be distributed through grants ranging from $5,000 to $50,000 each, depending on the size and scope of the business. Eligible businesses must have less than 50 employees and have experienced financial hardship due to the ongoing pandemic. The application process is set to open next month, and interested parties are encouraged to prepare the necessary documentation in advance. The aim of this initiative is to stimulate economic growth and help local businesses bounce back from the challenges brought on by the pandemic.

data.title: JetNet promotes Josh Baird to President and COO; leading provider of aviation data; Utica, NY; effective Jan 12, 2026.
data.description: In Utica, New York, on January 12, 2026, JETNET, a top aviation data and market insight provider, revealed the appointment of Josh Baird as the new President and Chief Operating Officer. Baird, formerly the Chief Operating Officer, has been promoted to this role and will maintain his reporting responsibilities.

data.title: Aeronero awarded Frost & Sullivan's 2025 Global Transformational Innovation Leadership for pioneering atmospheric water generation tech.
data.description: Aeronero, a company based in San Antonio, has been acknowledged by Frost & Sullivan for their innovative approach to water security. This recognition is for their pioneering hybrid atmospheric water generation technology that has proven to be a sustainable solution. The award was given in 2025 on a global level, showcasing the impact of Aeronero's work. This recognition highlights the importance of their breakthrough technology in addressing water shortages and the need for sustainable water solutions.

data.title: LumiNicole adds Linda Johansen-James and Fred Collins to Advisory Board; plans 40 automated stores in 2026.
data.description: Experienced professionals from the industry have recently been added to the Advisory Board of LumiNicole as the company gears up to open 40 automated retail stores and expand its alternative beauty and wellness marketplace across the country. The announcement was made in Las Vegas on January 12, 2026. LumiNicole is positioning itself as a next-generation player in the retail sector.

data.title: Toll Brothers introduces two new model homes in Camas, Washington. Grand opening on Jan 17, 2026.
data.description: The public is welcome to attend the grand opening event for the Brasada and Alderbook model homes in the Portland area on Saturday, January 17, 2026. This event marks the introduction of these housing options to potential buyers in the region. It presents an opportunity for interested parties to explore and evaluate these homes firsthand before making a decision. Join us on the specified date to experience the features and benefits of these new models and possibly find your dream home.
